The scenes are inspired by movies released in 1994-1999-2000-2009-2010-2019-2012-2015. The scenes have durations of 4-6-8-10-12 days. The director must ensure that no two consecutive scenes come from the same decade. The decades are {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. The movie years are 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015. The scenes from 1994 belong to {1990-1999}, scenes from 2004-2007 belong to {2000-2009}, and scenes from 2012-2015 belong to {2010-2019}. To minimize the total shooting days, the director should alternate scenes between different decades. The optimal sequence is 1994-2004-2012-2007-2015. This sequence ensures that no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade. The total shooting days for this sequence are 4-6-10-8-12 = 40 days. The total shooting days are minimized to 40 days while satisfying the decade constraint.

The director has a budget of 50 lakhs. The cost per day for the scenes is 1-1.5-2-2.5-3 lakhs. The goal is to maximize the number of scenes completed without exceeding the budget. The linear programming problem can be formulated as follows: maximize x1 + x2 + x3 + x4 + x5, subject to 1x1 + 1.5x2 + 2x3 + 2.5x4 + 3x5 <= 50, where x1, x2, x3, x4, x5 are the number of days each scene is shot and must be non-negative integers.
